_note: Feature tables for the mystery classes. n = feature name, d = one-line rules text the DM treats as authoritative.

1.d: 1/short rest, after examining a scene, person, or object, ask the DM one pointed question; the answer is a true, useful clue.
You recall any clue, name, date, or conversation from the current case verbatim.

2.d: Double proficiency bonus in two skills you are proficient with.

5.d: After 1 minute of conversation, learn whether a creature has lied to you during it.

6.d: 1/long rest, the DM tells you how two clues you hold relate, or that they do not.

7.d: Advantage on saves against fear and charm; con artists find you a hard mark.

9.d: You cannot be surprised while conscious; +2 initiative.

10.d: Double proficiency bonus in two more proficient skills.

11.d: Case Notes extends to every case and everything you have ever read.

13.d: 1/rest, an interrogation question must be answered truthfully or the subject visibly refuses (WIS save to refuse quietly).

14.d: In any settlement, an hour and a few coins buy you the local word on a person, place, or event.

15.d: Reaction when attacked: your read of the attacker imposes disadvantage, INT-mod uses per long rest.

17.d: Keen Deduction has 3 uses per short rest.

18.d: 1/long rest, reconstruct a past scene you visited in perfect detail; the DM answers three questions about it.

20.d: 1/long rest, name the culprit mechanism or motive; the DM confirms which parts of your theory are right.

3.d: On meeting a creature, learn its profession, recent whereabouts, or emotional state (pick one).
Street contacts in any city fetch, watch, or tail for you (DM adjudicates scope).

7.d: Add half your proficiency bonus to INT and WIS checks you are not proficient in.

11.d: 1/rest, grant yourself and allies within 30 ft advantage on the next check made in pursuit of a suspect.

15.d: Choose a named foe; advantage on all checks and saves opposing them until the case ends.

1.d: Double proficiency bonus on Insight; 1/rest, learn whether a creature is lying, afraid, or concealing something.
During a short rest, one creature you counsel regains an extra hit die of HP and ends one fear or charm effect.

2.d: Casts wizard-list spells as psychological treatments using INT.

3.d: After 10 minutes with a patient, learn their deepest current fear or fixation (the DM answers truthfully).

5.d: Allies within 10 ft add +1d4 to saves against fear and madness.

6.d: 1/long rest, a willing or restrained creature answers three questions truthfully under trance (WIS save resists if unwilling).

7.d: Advantage on saves against fear, charm, and madness.

9.d: 1/rest, end one charm, fear, or illusion effect on a creature within 30 ft.

10.d: Pick a creature you have treated or studied; advantage on Insight and Persuasion against it permanently.

11.d: Your Talking Cure works on two creatures per rest and also removes one level of exhaustion.

13.d: 1/long rest, read a sleeping or unconscious creature's dreams for three true impressions.

14.d: Immune to being frightened; your mind cannot be read unless you allow it.

15.d: Your Steadying Presence radius is 30 ft; allies in it are immune to madness effects.

17.d: 1/long rest, cure a creature of a long-term madness, phobia, or compulsion, including magical ones.

18.d: Hypnotic Induction needs only 1 minute and can be used on hostile creatures in conversation.

20.d: 1/long rest, cast one treatment of 5th level or lower without a slot; it cannot be resisted by creatures you have a Trauma Map of.

2.d: 1/rest, impose disadvantage on a creature's attack or check by voicing its doubt aloud (WIS save negates).
Advantage on checks to interpret dreams, art, ciphers, and ritual symbolism.

6.d: 1/long rest, an illusory double of a creature's guilt confronts it: it is frightened of you for 1 minute (WIS save negates).

10.d: 1/long rest, link the party's minds for 10 minutes: silent communication and shared Insight results.

14.d: 1/long rest, your treatments treat one target's saving throw as if it rolled a 1.

1.d: 1/short rest, reroll a failed Deception or Persuasion check.
10 minutes and small props give you a convincing new identity; Deception checks to hold it have advantage.

2.d: Double proficiency bonus in two skills you are proficient with.

5.d: Reaction: halve the damage of one attack that hits you.

6.d: After 1 minute of talk, learn what a creature most desires or most fears (pick one).

7.d: Disengage or Hide as a bonus action; escape grapples with advantage.

9.d: Maintain up to three full false identities with papers and reputations the DM treats as established.

10.d: Double proficiency bonus in two more proficient skills.

11.d: 1/turn, +2d6 damage against a creature that is charmed, deceived by you, or unaware you are a threat.

13.d: 1/long rest, when things go wrong, narrate a prepared escape (a bribed guard, a loose window); the DM makes it viable.

14.d: Advantage on saves against charm and against magic that would read your thoughts or force truth.

15.d: Silver Tongue recharges to 3 uses per short rest.

17.d: 1/long rest, a creature that can hear you treats you as a trusted friend for 10 minutes (WIS save negates).

18.d: Hide as a bonus action even in plain sight, 3/long rest.

20.d: 1/long rest, declare a con you have been running this session; the DM retroactively honors the setup within reason.

3.d: Plant a rumor in a settlement; within a day, most locals have heard and half believe it.
Advantage on Sleight of Hand; observers have disadvantage to catch you.

7.d: A creature that has believed one of your lies has disadvantage on Insight against you thereafter.

11.d: Hire and coordinate accomplices in any city; one favor per job is free.

15.d: 1/long rest, reveal a con at the perfect moment: every enemy who believed it is stunned until their next turn (WIS save negates).

1.d: Double proficiency bonus on Intimidation; creatures of your level or lower avoid provoking you.
Reaction when an adjacent ally is hit: take the hit instead.

2.d: Your unarmed strikes deal 1d6 + STR bludgeoning; you can grapple as a bonus action.

5.d: Attack twice when taking the Attack action.

6.d: Bodyguard works for allies within 10 ft; you move up to 10 ft to intercept.

7.d: 1/long rest, when you drop to 0 HP, drop to 1 instead.

9.d: 1/rest, force every hostile creature within 30 ft to save (WIS) or be frightened of you for 1 minute.

10.d: Resistance to bludgeoning damage from nonmagical attacks.

11.d: Attack three times when taking the Attack action.

13.d: Advantage against being moved, knocked prone, or grappled; your grapples escape at disadvantage.

14.d: After you drop a creature, its allies save (WIS) or are frightened of you until your next turn.

15.d: Allies within 10 ft gain +1 AC while you are conscious.

17.d: Hard to Kill recharges on a short rest.

18.d: Enemies treat all squares within 10 ft of you as difficult terrain.

20.d: While below half HP, resistance to all damage and your attacks deal +10 damage.

3.d: 1/rest, allies within 30 ft gain temp HP equal to your level + CON mod.
Advantage on Persuasion with laborers, dockhands, and the working poor; they will hide or help you.

7.d: Enemies leaving your reach provoke opportunity attacks even if they Disengage.

11.d: When you use Bodyguard, the attacker takes your STR mod + proficiency in damage.

15.d: 1/long rest, you and all allies within 30 ft each gain a reaction attack when any one of you is hit, for 1 minute.

1.d: Plausible papers and patter get you into crime scenes, offices, and functions ordinary citizens cannot enter (DM adjudicates).
1/rest, ask the DM who in the current scene knows something about your story; you get a true pointer.

2.d: You record conversations verbatim while appearing to doodle; double proficiency bonus on Investigation.

5.d: A creature you surprise with a question answers before thinking: one truthful sentence (WIS save negates).

6.d: Archives and old contacts yield the paper trail on any person or company within a day.

7.d: Reaction: impose disadvantage on one attack against you, CHA-mod uses per long rest.

9.d: Public figures hesitate: creatures who know you are press have disadvantage on Intimidation against you and think twice before violence.

10.d: Given a name or ledger, the DM tells you where the money actually went.

11.d: 1/long rest, publish or threaten to: a named organization must respond publicly within a day, opening a door for the party.

13.d: Magic and interrogation cannot pull your sources or notes from you unless you allow it.

14.d: Advantage on initiative; you cannot be surprised by creatures you have investigated.

15.d: Headline Instinct has 3 uses per rest and works on any topic, not just the current story.

17.d: On meeting a creature, learn whether it hides something that would ruin it (yes or no).

18.d: 1/long rest, a crowd within earshot acts on your words: blocks a street, boos a villain, shelters a fugitive.

20.d: 1/long rest, publish the truth: a named creature's public standing collapses, and its allies must save (WIS) or abandon it.

3.d: In any settlement, tip-collectors report to you: learn the week's notable arrivals, deaths, and deals.
Advantage on checks made under time pressure.

7.d: Send and receive coded messages between cities in hours, not days.

11.d: Your Front Page Pressure reaches every city on the wire; 2 uses per long rest.

15.d: 1/long rest, call in the full network: three true answers about any ongoing scheme, no matter how hidden.

1.d: 1/rest, commune with a spirit tied to a place, object, or body; it answers three brief questions truthfully as it understood things.
Bonus action: a shielding spirit grants you or an ally +2 AC until your next turn, WIS-mod uses per long rest.

2.d: Casts cleric-list spells as seance workings using WIS; the spirits are the conduit.

3.d: You sense any death that occurred in a room you enter, and roughly when and how violent.

5.d: 1/long rest, let a spirit speak through you: it converses with the party for 10 minutes.

6.d: You automatically detect spirits, possession, and haunted objects within 30 ft.

7.d: Resistance to necrotic damage; spirits cannot possess you unless you allow it.

9.d: Your Speak with the Departed can include the whole party as witnesses, and the spirit can manifest visibly.

10.d: 1/long rest, compel a spirit witness to relive its last hour aloud; the DM narrates what it saw.

11.d: A bound protective spirit follows you: 1/rest, it negates one attack that would hit you.

13.d: 1/long rest, lay a spirit to rest or release a possession without a contest; hostile entities get a CHA save.

14.d: See into the Ethereal Plane within 60 ft at will.

15.d: Spirit Ward can protect two creatures at once and also grants advantage on their next save.

17.d: Speak with the Departed works on spirits of any age and has 3 uses per rest.

18.d: 1/long rest, when a creature within 30 ft dies, hold its spirit: it can be revived within 24 hours as if freshly dead.

20.d: 1/long rest, call a procession of spirits: for 1 minute they obscure, terrify (WIS save), and strike (2d8 necrotic) as you direct.

1.d: 1/rest, glimpse a place, person, or event connected to a held object: one true image.

6.d: Reaction when hit: gain resistance to that instance of damage, WIS-mod uses per long rest.

10.d: 1/long rest, project your spirit up to 1 mile for 10 minutes; your body is helpless but warded.

14.d: Creatures you can see cannot become undead, and undead within 30 ft have disadvantage on saves against your workings.
